name: John the Ripper (Password Security Auditor)

auth.notes: Offline password auditing; no auth required

performance.notes: CPU/GPU optimized; slower than hashcat for GPU but broader format support

agent_readiness.notes: Classic password auditor/cracker. john hashes.txt (auto-detects format). john --wordlist=rockyou.txt hashes.txt. john --format=NT hashes.txt. john --show hashes.txt (display cracked). Helper tools: zip2john, rar2john, ssh2john, keepass2john, office2john (extract hashes from files). Jumbo version adds 100+ additional formats. --rules for mangling. CTF staple for hash extraction from encrypted files. Broader file format support than hashcat.

gotchas: Slower than hashcat for GPU workloads
Jumbo version (community) vs official version confusion
Auto-format detection can be wrong — specify --format explicitly
best_when: CTF hash extraction from encrypted files (zip2john, ssh2john, etc.)
CPU-only environments where hashcat GPU acceleration unavailable
Auto-detecting hash format from captured hashes
Password auditing with broader format support than hashcat
avoid_when: GPU cracking workloads (hashcat is significantly faster)
When hashcat supports the hash format (hashcat faster)
summary: John the Ripper is the classic password security auditor. Key strength: helper tools (zip2john, ssh2john, rar2john, keepass2john) extract hashes from encrypted files. Auto-detects hash format. Dictionary and rule-based attacks. Jumbo community version adds 100+ formats. CTF staple. Use hashcat for GPU speed; use John for file format extraction and CPU environments.
